title: Selinum turcomanicum
taxonomy: Selinum turcomanicum belongs to the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This plant is classified within the genus Selinum, which includes several species of parsley-like plants. At the species level, Selinum turcomanicum is a distinct species within this genus. Further classification places it within the tribe Apieae, which comprises several species of flowering plants. The full classification is: Apiaceae (family) > Selinum (genus) > S. turcomanicum (species).
uses: Selinum turcomanicum is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce swelling in the body. constituents: Selinum turcomanicum contains a variety of active constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones, which are known for their potential anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also rich in flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Additionally, Selinum turcomanicum contains essential oils, including limonene and beta-pinene, which contribute to its aromatic properties and may have antimicrobial effects. Other active constituents in the plant include phenolic acids and saponins, which have been reported to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. intro: Selinum turcomanicum is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae), native to Central Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as digestive issues, wounds, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Selinum turcomanicum include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Selinum turcomanicum is often used to prepare tinctures, infusions, and decoctions to treat a range of health issues, and is also used in some traditional recipes as a spice or culinary ingredient.
